name: portal
description: Mobile first batteries included web ui for sst/opencode. Git integration, in browser terminal, isolated workspaces.

Merge pull request #27 from nick-at-equalparts/fix/use-configured-hostname-for-opencode-client

fix: Use configured hostname instead of hardcoded localhost

commit.message:
fix: use configured hostname instead of hardcoded localhost

The OpenCode client was hardcoded to connect to localhost, which breaks
when OpenCode is bound to a specific interface (e.g., Tailscale IP).

This change reads the hostname from ~/.portal.json based on the port,
allowing Portal to connect to OpenCode servers on non-localhost addresses.

fix: hide model selector when agent is selected

commit.message:
fix: hide model selector when agent is selected

When an agent is selected, the model selector is now hidden since
agents already have their model configured in OpenCode config.

This also prevents sending the model parameter when an agent is
selected, letting the agent's configured model take precedence.

Fixes #24

refactor: remove PostHog analytics and update diff parsing to @pierre… (#17)

* refactor: remove PostHog analytics and update diff parsing to @pierre/diffs

* fix: address PR #17 code review feedback

- Extract Docker utilities to shared modules (packages/cli/src/docker.ts, apps/web/src/server/lib/docker.ts)
- Add proper error handling for all empty catch blocks
- Add validateMountPath() to block mounting sensitive system directories
- Make Docker image configurable via OPENCODE_DOCKER_IMAGE env var
- Fix parsePatchFiles API usage in app-sidebar to count files across all patches
- Fix ModelSelect vertical alignment by removing redundant sr-only Label
- Use AutoRemove: true for Docker containers

commit.message: docs: remove releases route, update navigation to Discord, update install and CLI docs
fix(docs): correct @vercel/react-router import path to /vite
chore: update turbo to ^2.7.2
chore(docs): update @vercel/react-router to latest (1.2.4)

feat(docs): add @vercel/react-router preset for proper Vercel deployment

- Add vercelPreset() to react-router.config.ts for SSR support
- Remove custom vercel.json, preset handles build configuration
fix(docs): remove api/og route from routes.ts

revert(docs): remove OG image generation API

- Remove /api/og endpoint and @takumi-rs/image-response dependency
- Simplify meta tags in home and docs pages
- Remove SSR external config from vite
chore: bump version to 0.1.23
chore: update bun.lock with new dependencies

refactor(web): minor UI polish and code cleanup

- Updated Link component styling
- Minor layout adjustments to pages

feat(web): improve file mention popover UI with better styling

- Refined visual design with backdrop blur and rounded corners
- Better selection highlighting with primary color
- Improved file icon styling and spacing

feat(web): add custom font family support with Inter, Geist Sans, Geist Mono

- Import Inter Variable, Geist Sans, and Geist Mono fonts
- CSS custom properties for font switching via data-font attribute
- Improved accent color selector styling

feat(web): redesign settings page with tabbed interface

- Settings organized into Appearance, Model, and API Keys tabs
- Font family selector with Inter, Geist Sans, Geist Mono, System
- Theme selector using dropdown instead of menu
- Placeholder for model selection and API key management
- Integrated breadcrumb navigation

feat(web): add breadcrumb context and new UI components

- BreadcrumbProvider context for dynamic page titles
- New Breadcrumbs, Tabs, and Separator UI components
chore(docs): remove placeholder tips-and-troubleshooting content
docs: update quickstart command to 'bunx openportal'

feat(docs): add releases page fetching from GitHub API

- New /releases route shows changelog timeline
- Parses PR titles from GitHub release body
- Minimalistic timeline UI with version badges
- Added Docs and Releases navigation links

feat(docs): add OG image generation API with Takumi

- New /api/og endpoint generates dynamic Open Graph images
- Simple template: title + description
- Release template: version + features list
- Updated meta tags to use local OG API

feat(web): add git diff viewer page with file count badge

- New /diff route displays git diff with syntax highlighting
- Custom FileDiffIcon component for sidebar
- useGitDiff hook fetches diff from OpenCode API
- Sidebar shows badge with count of changed files
- Diff parsed using react-diff-view library

feat(web): add git action buttons (Pull, Push, Create PR) to sidebar

- Pull button sends AI prompt to pull latest changes
- Push button sends AI prompt to commit and push changes
- Create PR button sends AI prompt to create PR using gh CLI
- All actions tracked with PostHog events

feat(web): add PostHog anonymous analytics tracking

- Add posthog-js integration with hardcoded keys for self-hosted app
- Disable autocapture and session recording for privacy
- Track session_created and diff_viewed events

feat(cli): add 'run' command for server-only mode without web UI

- openportal (default) starts OpenCode server + Web UI
- openportal run starts only OpenCode server (no Web UI)
- openportal list/ls shows running instances
- openportal stop terminates instances
- openportal clean removes stale entries
- Instance interface now supports port: number | null and webPid: number | null
feat: restore file mention feature with @ trigger in chat

fix: bundle web app with CLI and start both servers

readme_html:
OpenCode Portal

Disclaimer: This is a personal project and is not related to https://github.com/sst/opencode or the SST team. This portal is a personal-built interface for interacting with OpenCode instances.

A web-based UI for OpenCode, the AI coding agent. This portal provides a browser interface to interact with OpenCode sessions, view messages, and chat with the AI assistant.

Quick Start

Using bunx (Recommended)

The easiest way to run OpenCode Portal is using bunx:

# Navigate to your project directory
cd /path/to/your/project

# Run OpenCode Portal
bunx openportal

Note: OpenPortal works best when paired with Bun. Node.js may have some rough edges.

This will:

  • Start the OpenCode server (default port: 4000)
  • Start the web UI (default port: 3000)
  • Automatically find available ports if defaults are busy

Installation

You can also install globally:

bun install -g openportal

# Then run in any project directory
openportal

CLI Commands

openportal                    # Start OpenCode + Web UI
openportal run                # Start only OpenCode server (no Web UI)
openportal stop               # Stop running instances
openportal list               # List running instances
openportal clean              # Clean up stale entries

CLI Options

openportal [command] [options]

Options:
  -h, --help              Show help message
  -d, --directory <path>  Working directory (default: current directory)
  -p, --port <port>       Web UI port (default: 3000, auto-finds if busy)
  --opencode-port <port>  OpenCode server port (default: 4000, auto-finds if busy)
  --hostname <host>       Hostname to bind (default: 0.0.0.0)
  --name <name>           Instance name (default: directory name)

Prerequisites

OpenCode must be installed on your system. Install it using one of these methods:

# Using bun
bun install -g opencode

# Using Homebrew (macOS)
brew install sst/tap/opencode

Overview

OpenCode Portal connects to a running OpenCode server and provides:

  • Session management (create, view, delete sessions)
  • Real-time chat interface with the AI assistant
  • File mention support (@filename to reference files)
  • Model selection
  • Dark/light theme support

Why This Project?

OpenCode comes with its own official web UI that you can access by running:

opencode --port 4096

However, the official UI is currently under development and has some limitations:

  • Not mobile responsive
  • Limited mobile experience

This project was inspired by my personal need to access OpenCode from my mobile device when I don't have my laptop around. The goal is to provide a mobile-first, responsive interface for interacting with OpenCode instances remotely.

Use Case

This portal is designed for remote access to your OpenCode instance. Deploy the portal on a VPS alongside OpenCode, then use Tailscale (or similar VPN) to securely connect from your mobile device or any other machine.

Example setup:

[Your Phone] ---(Tailscale)---> [VPS running Portal + OpenCode]

Tech Stack

  • React Router - React framework
  • IntentUI - UI library
  • Tailwind CSS - Styling
  • Nitro - Server
  • OpenCode SDK - OpenCode API client

Contributing

Contributions are welcome! Here's how you can help:

Reporting Issues

  • Bugs: Report bugs by opening an issue with a clear description and steps to reproduce
  • Feature requests: Open an issue with the feature label and describe the proposed enhancement

Development Setup

  1. Fork the repository
  2. Clone your fork: 
    git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/portal.git
cd portal
  3. Install dependencies: 
    bun install
  4. Run the development server: 
    bun dev

Pull Request Process

  1. Create a feature branch from main
  2. Make your changes following the existing code style
  3. Test your changes thoroughly
  4. Update documentation if needed
  5. Submit a pull request with a clear description

Code Style

  • Use TypeScript for all new code
  • Follow the existing component patterns in apps/web/src/components/
  • Use Tailwind CSS for styling
  • Maintain consistent naming conventions
  • Add proper TypeScript types

Getting Help

  • Join our Discord community
  • Check existing issues before creating new ones
  • Join the discussion in existing issues
  • Be respectful and constructive in all interactions

License

MIT
